title: Time and Work — Advanced Module
objectives: Find one-day work and net work using rates
Solve combined worker and pipe problems
Apply alternate and sequential operation models
Use LCM and rate equations to simplify calculations
theory: Time and Work problems model a task as one unit of work.
One worker's daily work = 1 / total days required.
Combined work is the sum of individual rates when workers work together.
Outlet or leak work subtracts from the filling rate, creating net work.

formulas.explanation: If a worker does r units per day for t days, total work done is r × t.
A worker who finishes in T days does 1/T of the job in one day.

formulas.formula: Time for two workers = xy / (x + y)
formulas.explanation: When worker A takes x days and B takes y days, together they finish in xy/(x+y) days.

formulas.formula: Net rate = sum(inlet rates) - sum(outlet rates)
formulas.explanation: Use negative rates for emptying pipes or leaks.

workedExamples.title: Basic combined work
workedExamples.problem: A can complete a job in 10 days and B in 15 days. How long together?

workedExamples.title: Work with leak
workedExamples.problem: A pipe fills a tank in 8 hours. A leak empties it in 24 hours. How long if both are open?

workedExamples.title: Sequential worker problem
workedExamples.problem: A finishes a job in 12 days. B starts on day 4 and finishes the remaining work in 6 more days. How much of the work did A do?
steps: Work by A in first 3 days = 3 × 1/12 = 1/4.
Remaining work = 3/4; B completes it in 6 days => B's daily work = (3/4)/6 = 1/8.
Answer: A did 1/4 of the work and B's rate is 1/8 per day.

questions.question: If A completes a work in 10 days, his one day's work is:
questions.explanation: One-day work = 1 ÷ total days = 1/10.

questions.question: Work is equal to:
questions.explanation: Work is the product of rate and time taken.

questions.question: If A and B complete a work in 12 and 18 days respectively, together they take:
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/12 + 1/18 = 5/36 ⇒ time = 36/5 = 7.2 days.

questions.question: One day's work of a person taking N days is:
questions.explanation: If total job is 1 unit, daily work is reciprocal of days needed.

questions.question: Efficiency is directly proportional to:
questions.explanation: Efficiency measures work done per unit time, so it is directly proportional to work.

questions.question: Time is inversely proportional to:
questions.explanation: More efficient workers take less time to complete the same work.

questions.question: Which method is commonly used to avoid fractions?
questions.explanation: Using LCM of denominators simplifies fractional work rates.

questions.question: If one day's work is 1/25, total time required is:
questions.explanation: Total time is reciprocal of one-day work: 1 ÷ (1/25) = 25.

questions.question: The shortcut formula for two workers is:
questions.explanation: This formula gives the combined time when two workers with times x and y work together.

questions.question: The first step in solving Time and Work problems is:
questions.explanation: Converting tasks into daily work makes the rest of the solution systematic.

questions.question: A can finish a job in 16 days and B in 24 days. How many days will they take together?
questions.explanation: Combined work = 1/16 + 1/24 = 5/48, so time = 48/5 = 9.6 days.

questions.question: Pipe A fills a tank in 12 hours and pipe B fills it in 8 hours. How long will both pipes take together?
questions.explanation: Combined fill rate = 1/12 + 1/8 = 5/24, so time = 24/5 = 4.8 hours.

questions.question: A pipe fills a tank in 18 hours and a leak empties it in 24 hours. What is the net time to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Net rate = 1/18 - 1/24 = 1/72, so time = 72 hours.

questions.question: A, B, and C can complete a job in 10, 15, and 30 days respectively. How many days for all three working together?
questions.explanation: Total rate = 1/10 + 1/15 + 1/30 = 1/5, so time = 5 days.

questions.question: If A completes a job in 10 days and B is twice as efficient as A, how many days will B take?
questions.explanation: If B is twice as efficient, B takes half the time of A: 10 ÷ 2 = 5 days.

questions.question: A, B, and C can complete a job in 18, 12, and 9 days respectively. How long will they take if they all work together?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/18 + 1/12 + 1/9 = 1/4, so time = 4 days.

questions.question: A is twice as efficient as B. If A alone takes 18 days to finish a job, how many days will A and B take working together?
questions.explanation: A's rate = 2x and B's rate = x. If A takes 18 days, x = 1/36 and combined rate = 3/36 = 1/12.

questions.question: A can finish a job in 6 days and B in 3 days. If they work alternately for one day each starting with A, in how many days will the job be completed?
questions.explanation: In 2 days A and B do 1/6 + 1/3 = 1/2 of the work. In 4 days they complete the full job.

questions.question: A can finish a job in 20 days. B can finish the same job in 30 days. A works alone for 4 days and then B joins. How many more days are needed to complete the job?
questions.explanation: A does 4/20 = 1/5 work. Remaining 4/5 at combined rate 1/20 + 1/30 = 1/12 gives 4/5 ÷ 1/12 = 9.6 days.

questions.question: Pipe A fills a tank in 6 hours and pipe B fills it in 10 hours. A leak empties it in 15 hours. How many hours will it take to fill the tank with both pipes open?
questions.explanation: Net rate = 1/6 + 1/10 - 1/15 = 1/5, so time = 5 hours.

questions.question: A takes 12 days, B takes 15 days, and C takes 20 days to do the same work. How long will A and C take if they work together?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/12 + 1/20 = 2/15, so time = 15/2 = 7.5 days.

questions.question: A, B, and C start a job together. A and B work for 2 days and then C joins them, finishing the remaining work in 2 2/3 days. If A, B, and C take 12, 18, and 36 days individually, how long does the remaining work take after C joins?
questions.explanation: After 2 days, A and B do 2(1/12+1/18)=5/9. Remaining 4/9 at combined rate 1/12+1/18+1/36=1/6 takes (4/9)/(1/6)=8/3 days.

questions.question: A worker reduces his time by 20% after improving efficiency. If he originally took 30 days, how long will he take after improvement?
questions.explanation: Efficiency increases by 20%, so time decreases by factor 1.2: 30 ÷ 1.2 = 25 days.

questions.question: A worker finishes 1/3 of a job in 4 days. How many total days will he take to finish the full job?
questions.explanation: If 1/3 job takes 4 days, full job takes 4 × 3 = 12 days.

questions.question: Two workers can complete a task in 14 and 21 days respectively. How many days will they take if they work together?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/14 + 1/21 = 5/42, so time = 42/5 = 8.4 days.

questions.question: A can finish a job in 9 days and B in 12 days. If A works alone for 3 days and then B finishes the job alone, how many more days does B require?
questions.explanation: A does 3/9 = 1/3 of the work. Remaining 2/3 at B's rate 1/12 takes 8 days.

questions.question: If A finishes a job in 10 days and B finishes the same job in 15 days, what is the efficiency ratio A:B?
questions.explanation: Efficiency is inverse of time: A:B = 15:10 = 3:2.

questions.question: A, B, and C can do a job in 10, 20, and 30 days individually. How long will it take when they all work together?
A takes 18 days and B takes 9 days to finish the same work. They work alternately one day each starting with A. In how many days is the work completed?
questions.explanation: Each 2-day cycle completes 1/6 of the work. After 10 days they complete 5/6, then A works day 11 and B finishes on day 12.

questions.question: A, B, and C can complete a job in 6, 12, and 24 days respectively. If each works for 2 days, what fraction of the job is done?

practiceProblems.question: A person can finish a job in 12 days. After 4 days, another person with twice the efficiency joins. How many more days are required?
practiceProblems.explanation: First person does 4/12=1/3 work in 4 days. Remaining 2/3 done at combined rate 1/12+2/12=1/4 ⇒ time=8/3=2 2/3 days.
practiceProblems.shortcut: Convert efficiency to one-day work and subtract work done.

practiceProblems.question: A tank is filled by pipe A in 10 hours and pipe B in 15 hours. After 3 hours of both pipes working, pipe B is closed. How long will A take to finish the remaining work?
practiceProblems.explanation: Combined work in 3h = 3*(1/10+1/15)=1/2. Remaining 1/2 by A alone at 1/10 = 5 hours.
practiceProblems.shortcut: Use combined rate first, then remaining work / single rate.

references: BRM Time and Work content standard
